name: Super-WIMP / gravitino dark matter
summary: The gravitino (spin-3/2 superpartner of the graviton) is the LSP, with mass set by the SUSY-breaking scale. The next-to-lightest superpartner (NLSP) decays slowly into the gravitino + SM particle, populating the DM relic. Super-weak gravitational coupling (1/M_Pl) makes the gravitino effectively non-thermal; abundance is set by NLSP freeze-out + decay or by thermal scattering in the early universe.

addresses_puzzles.note: Naturally light DM candidate (keV-GeV) consistent with super-WIMP scaling; relic abundance Omega_DM ~ (m_3/2 / 1 keV)(T_R / 10^9 GeV) sets reheating-temperature constraints. BBN bounds on NLSP decays exclude regions but leave keV-MeV gravitino windows.
Long-lived gravitino as super-WIMP DM: NLSP decay during BBN injects photons that destroy Li-7 in the required range. The mechanism predicts a specific correlation between Omega_DM and tau_NLSP.

touches_benchmarks.note: Late NLSP decays inject electromagnetic energy / mesons during BBN; tight bounds on tau_NLSP > 10^4 s and EM energy injection.

introduces.note: Builds on SUSY framework. New free parameters are m_3/2 and reheating temperature T_R.

history: Pagels and Primack (1982), 'Supersymmetry, cosmology, and new physics at TeraElectronvolt energies', Phys. Rev. Lett. 48, 223 — first proposed gravitino DM. Subsequently developed in GMSB and AMSB frameworks; modern bounds from BBN + CMB constrain reheating temperature.

references.citation: Pagels and Primack, 'Supersymmetry, cosmology, and new physics at TeraElectronvolt energies', Phys. Rev. Lett. 48, 223 (1982)
